title: Implement HTTP endpoints, mountIndexerRoutes, and HttpIndexerClient
markdown:
## Goal

Create HTTP endpoints for the indexer system and a client that consumes them. This enables remote querying: a web app can query an indexer running on a different server.

## Background

The indexer backend runs server-side. Web and mobile apps need HTTP access to query, search, and aggregate indexed data. The endpoints follow the existing B3nd server pattern from `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts`.

The `HttpIndexerClient` extends the concept of `NodeProtocolInterface` with query/search/aggregate methods, giving apps a unified client for both key-value and indexed access.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `mountIndexerRoutes(app: MinimalRouter, backend: IndexerBackend, options?)` function that adds routes:
  - `POST /api/v1/indexer/query` -- accepts `IndexQuery` body, returns `IndexQueryResult`
  - `POST /api/v1/indexer/search` -- accepts `{ text, options? }` body, returns `IndexQueryResult`
  - `POST /api/v1/indexer/aggregate` -- accepts `{ pipeline: AggregationStage[] }` body, returns `AggregationResult`
  - `GET /api/v1/indexer/health` -- returns indexer health status
  - `GET /api/v1/indexer/feed?since={cursor}&limit={n}` -- returns feed entries
  - `GET /api/v1/indexer/views` -- returns available view keys metadata (not the keys themselves)
- [ ] All endpoints return proper error responses (400 for bad input, 500 for server errors)
- [ ] `HttpIndexerClient` class:
  - Constructor accepts `{ url: string; headers?: Record<string, string> }`
  - `query(query: IndexQuery): Promise<IndexQueryResult>`
  - `search(text: string, options?: SearchOptions): Promise<IndexQueryResult>`
  - `aggregate(pipeline: AggregationStage[]): Promise<AggregationResult>`
  - `health(): Promise<IndexerHealthStatus>`
  - `feed(since?: string, limit?: number): Promise<FeedResult>`
- [ ] Client handles network errors gracefully (returns error result, not throws)
- [ ] Tests: round-trip query/search/aggregate through HTTP server and client

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts` for the `MinimalRouter` pattern and route conventions
2. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/http.ts`:
   - `mountIndexerRoutes(app, backend, options?)`:
     - Parse JSON bodies, validate required fields
     - Call backend methods
     - Return JSON responses
   - Export route mounting function (composable with existing httpServer)
3. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/client.ts`:
   - `HttpIndexerClient` that uses `fetch()` to call the HTTP endpoints
   - Each method: POST with JSON body, parse response, handle errors
4. Create `libs/b3nd-indexer/http_test.ts`:
   - Use in-memory backend + Hono app for testing
   - Seed data, mount routes, test each endpoint
   - Test round-trip: seed -> mount -> client.query() -> verify results
5. Export from `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts`

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-indexer/http.ts` -- new: mountIndexerRoutes and HTTP route handlers
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/client.ts` -- new: HttpIndexerClient
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/http_test.ts` -- new: integration tests
- `libs/b3nd-indexer/mod.ts` -- add exports

## Testing

```bash
deno test --allow-all libs/b3nd-indexer/http_test.ts
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A6-06 (query), G1-A6-07 (aggregation), G1-A6-08 (view keys for /views endpoint)
- **Blocks**: G1-A6-10 (React hooks consume the client)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Implement `IndexerDefinition` interface and runtime
- [ ] Implement `IndexerBackend` — at least one backend (SQLite or Postgres)
- [ ] Implement `createIndexerProcessor` — compose `Processor` that pipes messages to indexer
- [ ] Implement message feed / event stream (pull-based cursor, `GET /api/v1/feed?since={cursor}`)
- [ ] Implement backfill mechanism (paginate through source node, replay through indexer)
- [ ] Implement cursor store (using B3nd node itself as storage)
- [ ] Implement `
